answer:
To neutralize gravitational attraction, the electrostatic repulsion must equal the gravitational force:  
\[
\frac{k q^2}{r^2} = \frac{G M_E M_M}{r^2}
\]  
so  
\[
q = \sqrt{\frac{G M_E M_M}{k}}.
\]  
Using \(G = 6.67 \times 10^{-11}\), \(M_E = 5.98 \times 10^{24}\,\text{kg}\), \(M_M = 7.35 \times 10^{22}\,\text{kg}\), and \(k = 8.99 \times 10^9\), the result is approximately \(5.7 \times 10^{13}\,\text{C}\).

\boxed{H}
